creator: jrtc27@freebsd.org
text: If you follow it back, normally on FreeBSD we (confusingly) use fpu-glibc.h for cpu-target.h, but that requires feenableexcept to exist. However, riscv64 has feenableexcept as a static inline function in its fenv.h, and GCC's configure scripts aren't prepared for that, so they think it doesn't exist, fall back on fpu-sysv.h and then the build dies because riscv64 (like every non-x86 architecture) doesn't implement either of fp(re)setsticky.
